tasks.subject: [pip-mobile-sheet] [P1] Add overlay opt-out to DrawerContent
tasks.description: In apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/drawer.tsx, add an optional `overlay?: boolean` prop (default true) to DrawerContent. When false, skip rendering <DrawerOverlay /> inside the portal; everything else (handle div, classes, forwardRef style — keep forwardRef, this is an edit to an existing forwardRef file) is unchanged. TSDoc the prop: non-modal sheets (<Drawer modal={false}>) pass false so the page behind stays interactive and undimmed (the mobile PIP sheet). z-index/margin overrides ride the existing className seam (cn caller-last + tailwind-merge) — do NOT add props for them. Unit tests in layers/shared/ui/__tests__/drawer.test.tsx (create if absent, reuse the vaul-in-jsdom setup from layers/widgets/agents/__tests__/AgentsPage.test.tsx if shims are needed): (a) default render inside an open Drawer produces an overlay element (assert via its fixed inset-0 class or a data-slot if you add one), (b) overlay={false} produces none, (c) children still render. Existing consumers pass nothing and must stay green.

tasks.subject: [pip-mobile-sheet] [P1] Build PipSheet, the mobile PIP presenter
tasks.description:
New file apps/client/src/layers/features/pip-panel/ui/PipSheet.tsx. Props: { content: PipContent; onClose: () => void; children: React.ReactNode }. Compose ONLY from the @/layers/shared/ui barrel (Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerTitle; icon X from lucide-react) — no direct vaul import in the feature. Structure: { if(!open) onClose(); }} modal={false} shouldScaleBackground={false} snapPoints={SNAP_POINTS as unknown as (number|string)[]} activeSnapPoint={snap} setActiveSnapPoint={setSnap}> with const SNAP_POINTS = [0.5, 0.94] and useState initial SNAP_POINTS[0] (opens at peek; snap state component-local, resets every open by design). DrawerContent gets overlay={false} and className="z-40 mt-0 h-full max-h-[94%]" (tailwind-merge overrides the wrapper's z-50 mt-24 h-auto). Inside: header row (border-b, px-4, truncated DrawerTitle text-sm font-medium showing content.title; type=button close button aria-label="Close" with X size-4, focus-ring, calling onClose) then body
{children}
. If Radix warns about a missing description in tests, add aria-describedby={undefined} on DrawerContent. dismissible stays default true — drag below peek fires onOpenChange(false) → onClose. TSDoc: non-modal invariants (modal false + overlay false + shouldScaleBackground false — the page behind must stay interactive; PIP is ambient at z-40, deliberately below every z-50 modal surface) and dismiss-= -close being safe under dual-live. Unit tests features/pip-panel/__tests__/PipSheet.test.tsx: renders title and children; X click calls onClose; simulate onOpenChange(false) → onClose called. Keep the component <300 lines, no forwardRef (new component, React 19 ref-as-prop rules).

tasks.subject: [pip-mobile-sheet] [P1] GATE: validate vaul non-modal snap mechanics in the playground
tasks.description: Validation gate (ideation D8/spec Phase 1): with PipHost branch wired (or a temporary showcase harness), open the demo PIP content in a browser at a mobile viewport (~390x844) via the dev playground (/dev route, PipPanelShowcases) and verify: (1) sheet opens at the 0.5 peek snap; (2) the page behind remains scrollable and clickable (non-modal, no overlay, no body scroll-lock leakage); (3) drag handle up reaches the 0.94 snap; (4) drag down past peek dismisses (onOpenChange false fires); (5) no background scaling (shouldScaleBackground false); (6) content taps inside the sheet work. If ANY of these fail in a way that cannot be fixed with vaul props, STOP the batch and re-plan against the recorded contingency (modal drawer + minimized pill, ideation §5 option 3) before building further. Record the validation outcome (pass/fail + notes) in specs/pip-mobile-sheet/04-implementation.md as it is created later — at minimum leave notes in the PR description.

tasks.subject: [pip-mobile-sheet] [P2] Presenter branch in PipHost + DesktopPip extraction
tasks.description: Restructure apps/client/src/layers/features/pip-panel/ui/PipHost.tsx: PipHost body becomes — read pipContent, closePip, useIsMobile; if pipContent===null return null; if isMobile return <PipSheet content={pipContent} onClose={closePip}>{renderPipContent(pipContent)}</PipSheet>; else return <DesktopPip content={pipContent} onClose={closePip} />. New internal component DesktopPip (same file, module scope, NOT exported from the barrel): absorbs verbatim today's desktop-only concerns — pipGeometry/setPipGeometry selectors, dockGeometry state + defaultGeometry + sameGeometry + the resize-repin effect, AnimatePresence + FloatingPanel key="pip-panel" + the existing omitted-onRestore comment block. DELETE: the close-on-cross effect (currently lines 156-158: if (isMobile && pipContent!==null) closePip()) and the `if (isMobile) return null` guard (line 161). NOTE the AnimatePresence contract: with the early `pipContent===null` return, the desktop exit animation would break (AnimatePresence must stay mounted while its child leaves). Preserve it: keep PipHost returning <AnimatePresence> with DesktopPip's FloatingPanel conditionally inside — i.e. desktop path must keep the ALWAYS-MOUNTED AnimatePresence wrapper exactly as today (restructure so the null-return applies only to the mobile branch, or keep the existing conditional-inside-AnimatePresence shape for desktop and mount the sheet branch separately). The sheet branch has no exit animation in v1 (accepted, spec §3): host unmounts it when content clears; drag-down provides its own motion. Update the PipHost TSDoc: mobile guard paragraph is replaced by presenter-swap semantics (content survives breakpoint crossings; geometry is desktop-only). Extend features/pip-panel/__tests__/PipHost.test.tsx (mock useIsMobile per existing patterns): mobile+content → sheet presenter mounted, FloatingPanel absent; desktop+content → FloatingPanel mounted, sheet absent; flip isMobile true→false and false→true with content open → content STILL open and the other presenter mounted (regression on the deleted close-on-cross); null content renders neither in both modes; desktop geometry behavior tests stay green unchanged.

tasks.subject: [pip-mobile-sheet] [P3] Enable WidgetFence pop-out on touch
tasks.description: In apps/client/src/layers/features/gen-ui/ui/WidgetFence.tsx: change the rendering gate at line ~105 from `sessionId && !isMobile` to `sessionId` only; remove the now-unused useIsMobile hook call and import (keep hook-order comment accurate — openPip is still called unconditionally). Button visibility classes change from `opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100` to `opacity-100 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100` keeping `focus-visible:opacity-100` — always visible below md (no hover on touch), hover-reveal preserved at md+. Update the sibling comment: it currently says 'Hidden below 768px, where the PIP host renders nothing' — now it should say the button is always visible on touch (no hover) and hover-revealed on desktop; the host now presents a bottom sheet below 768px (DOR-299). aria-label stays 'Pop out into a floating window'. Update __tests__: the existing hidden-on-mobile assertion inverts (button renders when useIsMobile mocked true); keep absent-without-sessionId.

tasks.subject: [pip-mobile-sheet] [P3] Enable McpAppBlock pop-out on mobile
tasks.description: In apps/client/src/layers/features/mcp-apps/ui/McpAppBlock.tsx: remove the `!isMobile` guard around the pop-out button (~line 91) so it always renders (it is already always-visible styling — no class changes needed); remove useIsMobile usage/import if nothing else uses it in the file. Update the comment above it ('Hidden below 768px: the PIP host renders nothing there') to reflect the sheet presenter (DOR-299). No bridge changes — pip is already advertised and granted unconditionally (model/bridge.ts:29,137). Update __tests__ if any assert the mobile-hidden behavior; add/extend a test that the pop-out button renders when useIsMobile is mocked true.

tasks.subject: [pip-mobile-sheet] [P3] Docs, changelog fragment, showcase note
tasks.description: Three small artifacts. (1) Docs: in docs/guides/generative-ui.mdx and docs/integrations/mcp-server.mdx, extend the existing one-line PIP mentions with the phone behavior — plain language per the writing-for-humans skill, e.g. 'On phones, the popped-out view docks to a bottom sheet you can drag up to play or down to dismiss.' One sentence each, no hype. (2) Changelog: ONE fragment changelog/unreleased/<timestamp-id>-mobile-pip-bottom-sheet.md (allocate the id with node .claude/scripts/id.ts; follow the existing fragment format in changelog/unreleased/ — read a recent one first, e.g. 260711-183335-live-widgets-in-pip.md). Plain-language: PIP now works on phones as a bottom sheet; widgets and MCP apps stay live and playable; drag down to dismiss. If the changelog populator hook amends an auto-stub into the feat commit, consolidate: keep only the real fragment, git rm the stub && git commit --amend --no-edit. (3) Showcase: in apps/client/src/dev/showcases/PipPanelShowcases.tsx add a short mobile note (narrow the viewport below 768px to exercise the bottom-sheet presenter; the demo button works in both modes).
tasks.activeForm: Writing docs, changelog, and showcase note

tasks.subject: [pip-mobile-sheet] [P4] Minimized mini-bar state + layout dock hook
tasks.description: Per spec Amendment 2 (read it in full; it is the binding contract): pip slice gains ephemeral pipMinimized + minimizePip/restorePip (openPip/closePip reset false); PipHost mobile branch renders PipMiniBar when minimized (AnimatePresence keys pip-minibar/pip-sheet); PipSheet gains onMinimize prop — drag-below-peek now minimizes instead of closing, chevron-down button (aria-label Minimize) added before X; new PipMiniBar.tsx (h-16 fixed bottom z-40, role=complementary, full-width restore button with pulse dot + truncated title + ChevronUp, separate X close); mini-bar sets --pip-dock: 64px on documentElement while mounted, AppShell.tsx + App.tsx main content wrappers consume paddingBottom var(--pip-dock, 0px); desktop-to-mobile crossing with content open lands minimized (isMobile rising-edge effect). Tests per Amendment 2 test list. Docs sentence + changelog fragment mention minimize.
